title: digitalis lutea subsp. transiens
intro: Digitalis lutea subsp. transiens, also known as a subspecies of the foxglove plant, is a type of flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate the heart and lower blood pressure. The health benefits of Digitalis lutea subsp. transiens include its potential to treat conditions such as congestive heart failure and atrial fibrillation, as well as its use as a diuretic to help reduce fluid retention. Herbal preparations of the plant, typically derived from the leaves and flowers, can be found in various forms, including tinctures, capsules, and teas.
